name: Inspiratory pause time
description: Inspiratory pause time — duration of the end-inspiratory no-flow hold, in seconds.

en.name: Inspiratory pause time
en.shortDescription: Inspiratory pause time — duration of the end-inspiratory no-flow hold, in seconds.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Inspiratory pause time is the duration of the no-flow hold at the end of inspiration (the inspiratory plateau/pause), during which plateau pressure is measured. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, set or measured by the ventilator. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in seconds (s).

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for this duration. All are reported in seconds.

- *4353623 / 250821007 — Inspiratory pause time (SNOMED)*
- *36303637 / 76227-8 — Inspiratory pause time Ventilator (LOINC)*
- *36303489 / 76226-0 — Inspiratory pause time setting Ventilator (LOINC)*

**Note on units**: reported in **seconds** (`8555 / s — second`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when not otherwise qualified: *4353623 / 250821007 — Inspiratory pause time*.
- **Setting vs measured**: map the set value to the LOINC 'setting' concept and the measured value to the measured concept; use the generic SNOMED concept when the source does not distinguish them.
- This is one specific breath-phase duration; inspiratory time, expiratory time and inspiratory pause time are separate concept sets (separate variables).

fr.name: Temps de pause inspiratoire
fr.shortDescription: Temps de pause inspiratoire — durée de la pause télé-inspiratoire sans débit, en secondes.
